profile.name: Yogendra Koyelada
profile.summary: I am a Business Intelligence Developer with 3.5 years of experience building actionable data solutions. I specialize in designing high-impact interactive dashboards in Power BI, writing optimized SQL databases, and developing sophisticated DAX measures that translate raw data into strategic business decisions.

career.title: SQL Developer
career.description: Focused on database development, ETL pipelines, and performance tuning.

career.title: Power BI Developer
career.description: Built interactive dashboards, customized KPI cards, and self-service reporting.

career.title: Business Intelligence Developer
career.description: End-to-end BI solutions including modeling, automation, and advanced DAX integration.

projects.title: Insurance Claims & Premium Dashboard
projects.businessProblem: Operations lacked real-time visibility into claim ratios, agent performance, and premium collection metrics. Manual reporting delayed the detection of SLA breaches in settlement pipelines.

projects.title: Sales Performance & Strategy Platform
projects.businessProblem: Sales executives struggled to model custom discounts dynamically during low-sales periods, resulting in missed quotas and slow reaction to regional sales dips.

experience.title: Business Intelligence Developer (Insurance Analytics)
experience.description: Designed and optimized comprehensive Power BI reporting systems for claims operations, premium collections, and agent performance. Developed custom KPIs tracking claim ratios, claims payment timelines, settlement percentages, and renewals. Engineered agent performance comparison tables that allowed management to analyze premium contributions and speed up claims processing. Focused heavily on report user experience and immediate information delivery to reduce SLA breaches.

experience.title: Business Intelligence Developer (Sales & Strategy)
experience.description: Created interactive sales performance platforms with regional comparisons and quota tracking. Implemented dynamic parameter selections and 'what-if' analyses for discount structures during sales declines, empowering sales leaders to make strategic promotion decisions. Extracted strategic insights to identify low-performance periods, prompting the introduction of new offers that recovered revenue dips and improved overall profit margins.

certifications.description: Validated skills in designing data models, building reports, configuring security (RLS), and deploying enterprise dashboard solutions in the Power BI Service.

certifications.description: Certified in writing optimized queries, designing views, database indexes, stored procedures, and implementing CTE-based analytics.

sql.title: Data Retrieval with CTEs & Window Functions
sql.description: Leveraged Common Table Expressions (CTEs) to simplify multi-step aggregations. Applied DENSE_RANK() and SUM() window functions with PARTITION BY to compute per-region monthly sales rankings and running revenue totals — enabling trend analysis without subqueries.
sql.code:
-- Calculate monthly sales rank within each region and dynamic running totals
WITH MonthlySalesCTE AS (
  SELECT 
    r.RegionName,
    DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(s.OrderDate), MONTH(s.OrderDate), 1) AS SalesMonth,
    SUM(s.Revenue) AS MonthlyRevenue,
    COUNT(s.OrderID) AS TotalOrders
  FROM FactSales s
  INNER JOIN DimRegion r ON s.RegionID = r.RegionID
  WHERE s.OrderStatus = 'Completed'
  GROUP BY r.RegionName, YEAR(s.OrderDate), MONTH(s.OrderDate)
)
SELECT 
  RegionName,
  SalesMonth,
  MonthlyRevenue,
  -- Rank sales performance of months per region
  DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY RegionName ORDER BY MonthlyRevenue DESC) as MonthRank,
  -- Running total of revenue over time for each region
  SUM(MonthlyRevenue) OVER (
    PARTITION BY RegionName 
    ORDER BY SalesMonth 
    ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
  ) as RunningTotalRevenue
FROM MonthlySalesCTE;

sql.title: Database Optimization: Views & Indexing
sql.description: Created a schema-bound view on the claims settlement table and materialized it using a unique clustered index — significantly reducing Power BI query load times. Added a non-clustered covering index on frequently-joined foreign key columns.
sql.code:
-- Create a materializable view for claims summary to simplify Power BI connectivity
CREATE VIEW dbo.vw_ClaimSettlementPerformance
WITH SCHEMABINDING -- Bind schema for creating clustered index
AS
SELECT 
  c.ClaimID,
  c.PolicyID,
  c.ClaimStatus,
  c.ReportedDate,
  c.ClaimAmount,
  DATEDIFF(day, c.ReportedDate, c.SettledDate) as SettlementDays
FROM dbo.FactClaims c
WHERE c.ClaimStatus = 'Settled';
GO

-- Create a unique clustered index on the view to materialize it and speed up reads
CREATE UNIQUE CLUSTERED INDEX IX_vw_ClaimSettlementPerformance_ClaimID 
ON dbo.vw_ClaimSettlementPerformance (ClaimID);

-- Create a non-clustered index on FactClaims foreign keys to optimize joins
CREATE NONCLUSTERED INDEX IX_FactClaims_PolicyID_ClaimStatus
ON dbo.FactClaims (PolicyID, ClaimStatus)
INCLUDE (ClaimAmount, ReportedDate, SettledDate);

sql.title: Stored Procedures: ETL & Data Automation
sql.description: Built a parameterized stored procedure for aggregating monthly claim performance metrics with full error handling via TRY/CATCH. Accepts date range and minimum settlement amount as inputs, enabling flexible reporting automation and Power BI dataset refresh pipelines.

dax.title: Claim Ratio (CALCULATE & FILTER)
dax.description: Computes the net claim ratio by dividing total settled/approved claim amounts against total premium using CALCULATE with ALL() to remove slicer context. Used in executive dashboards to monitor portfolio health in real time.
dax.code:
Claim Ratio = 
VAR TotalClaims = 
    CALCULATE(
        SUM(FactClaims[ClaimAmount]),
        FILTER(
            ALL(FactClaims[ClaimStatus]),
            FactClaims[ClaimStatus] = "Settled" || FactClaims[ClaimStatus] = "Approved"
        )
    )
VAR TotalPremium = 
    CALCULATE(
        SUM(FactPolicies[PremiumAmount]),
        ALL(FactPolicies[PolicyStatus])
    )
RETURN
    DIVIDE(TotalClaims, TotalPremium, 0)

dax.title: Dynamic KPI Selector (SWITCH & SELECTEDVALUE)
dax.description: Powers a field-parameter-driven KPI card that dynamically switches between Revenue, Claims, Loss Ratio, and SLA Breaches based on user slicer selection. Eliminates the need for multiple separate measures on the report canvas.
dax.code:
Selected Metric Value = 
VAR SelectedMetric = SELECTEDVALUE(DimMetricSelector[MetricName], "Revenue")
RETURN
    SWITCH(
        SelectedMetric,
        "Revenue", SUM(FactSales[Revenue]),
        "Claims", SUM(FactClaims[ClaimAmount]),
        "Loss Ratio", [Claim Ratio],
        "SLA Breaches", COUNTROWS(FILTER(FactClaims, FactClaims[SettlementDays] > 30)),
        BLANK()
    )

dax.title: Dynamic Title for KPI Cards (Interactivity)
dax.description: Generates context-aware titles for report visuals that update automatically based on region and date slicer selections. Improves report readability and eliminates manual title editing when sharing reports across business units.
dax.code:
Dynamic KPI Title = 
VAR SelectedRegion = SELECTEDVALUE(DimRegion[RegionName], "All Regions")
VAR MinSelectedDate = MIN('Date'[Date])
VAR MaxSelectedDate = MAX('Date'[Date])
VAR FormatStart = FORMAT(MinSelectedDate, "MMM YYYY")
VAR FormatEnd = FORMAT(MaxSelectedDate, "MMM YYYY")
RETURN
    "Performance for " & SelectedRegion & " (" & FormatStart & " - " & FormatEnd & ")"
